_metadata.description: make_orchestrator_agent skill — generates orchestrator agent specs that delegate to specialist subagents. Sibling to make_agent (which generates individual agents) and make_AGENTS (which generates project context files).
_metadata.instructions: MD = the why (mission, topology rationale, routing design, platform coverage). JSON = the what (templates, schemas, validation rules, compact_boilerplate). Update both files together.

companion_files.qc_skill: make_agent_qc.md (ORCH-QC family extends make_agent_qc; see qc_checks below)

skill_details.description: Generates orchestrator agent specs (.md + .json pair) whose role is to delegate work to specialist subagents via auto-generated delegate_to_<specialist> tools.

platform_grounding._description: Source docs that ground this skill's design. See source_docs/ for cached copies.

agent_type.description: Workflow-based skill: gather inputs → read specialist specs → synthesize delegate_to_* tools → propose orchestrator spec → wait for confirmation → write files → run make_agent_qc.

behavioral_discipline._description: make_orchestrator_agent operates under the behavioral discipline. Source: knowledge/behavioral_discipline.md/.json.

_skipped_principles_rationale.P-005: Single-step workflow (one orchestrator spec per invocation). Decomposition does not apply.

inputs.name: orchestrator_purpose
inputs.format: One-line description of what the orchestrator coordinates
inputs.example: Coordinates a researcher and a writer to produce documented summaries on technical topics.

inputs.name: specialist_paths
inputs.format: Array of file paths to specialist .json spec files (each must already exist; minimum 2, maximum 10)

inputs.name: termination_criteria
inputs.format: Observable condition under which the orchestrator stops delegating and returns its synthesis. Must be expressible as a function of conversation state.
inputs.example: All required specialists have returned and the user's original question has a synthesized answer.

inputs.name: interaction_pattern
inputs.format: Interaction pattern for the GENERATED orchestrator. Pulled from the standard decision flow in make_agent.md → '## Behavioral Discipline (core)'. Most orchestrators are multi_step_batch or conversational.

inputs.name: io_mode
inputs.format: I/O mode for the generated orchestrator's input contract — same enum as make_agent's io_contract.inputs[0].type

inputs.name: file_filter
inputs.format: Glob pattern when io_mode=folder. Empty otherwise.

outputs.name: orchestrator_md
outputs.format: Generated <orchestrator_name>.md at the repo root (or a path the user specifies)
outputs.conditions: Created if absent. If a file at the target path exists, the skill proposes a diff and waits for confirmation (P-002). Never silently overwrites.

io_contract.side_effects: Creates or updates two files (.md + .json) after user confirmation. Reads specialist .json files at generation time (no writes to them). Does NOT modify make_agent_qc, make_agent, or any other skill file.

orchestrator_md_required_sections._description: Sections that EVERY generated orchestrator MD must contain, in this order. Used by the skill to generate and by ORCH-QC to validate.

sections.heading: # <Orchestrator Name>
sections.description: H1 with orchestrator name + one-line description below.

sections.heading: ## Mission
sections.description: What the orchestrator coordinates, why, who uses it.

sections.heading: ## Topology
sections.description: List of specialists and their purpose (one bullet each, mirroring topology.specialists in the JSON).

sections.heading: ## Routing Decisions
sections.description: When to delegate to which specialist; failure-route fallbacks if any.

sections.heading: ## Cross-Agent State
sections.description: What the orchestrator carries across delegations vs. what each specialist sees.

sections.heading: ## Termination
sections.description: Restates the termination_criteria from the JSON; references max_turns backstop.

sections.heading: ## Behavioral Discipline
sections.description: Standard discipline block substituted from knowledge/behavioral_discipline.json → compact_boilerplate.md_section_template for the orchestrator's interaction_pattern.

orchestrator_md_optional_sections._description: Sections to include only when the inclusion criteria apply.

sections.heading: ## Platform Notes
sections.include_when: The orchestrator is intended for a specific platform with non-default behavior (e.g., xAI-specific deployment, Google ADK workflow agents like SequentialAgent).

sections.heading: ## Failure Routing
sections.include_when: The orchestrator has fallback specialists (e.g., primary fails → secondary). Document the failure-detection signal and the fallback target.

sections.heading: ## Examples
sections.include_when: Always recommended; only omit if the orchestrator is trivial. Show one happy-path delegation flow and one edge case.

topology_schema._description: Schema for the `topology` object in the generated orchestrator JSON. ORCH-QC validates against this schema.

name.format: snake_case identifier; used to derive delegate_to_<name> tool name

spec_path.format: Relative or absolute path to specialist .json; must exist at generation time

purpose.format: One-line description of what the specialist does and when to delegate to it

input_contract.format: Schema for the task argument the orchestrator passes; defaults to { task: string } if specialist takes string

expected_output.format: Brief description of what the specialist returns; informs orchestrator post-processing

routing_strategy.description: v1: llm_routed is the only supported strategy. declarative and hybrid are reserved for future versions.

carry.description: What context flows orchestrator → specialist with each delegation

termination_criteria.format: Non-empty observable condition. Must be expressible as a function of conversation state.

delegate_tool_template._description: Template for the delegate_to_<specialist> tool emitted in the orchestrator's tools array. One tool per specialist in topology.specialists. The `description` field carries the routing guidance — its quality is critical (Pitfall #7 in make_agent.md).

template.name: delegate_to_{{specialist_name}}
template.description: Use this tool to delegate to the {{specialist_name}} specialist. {{specialist_purpose}} Use it when: {{when_to_use}}. Do NOT use it for: {{when_not_to_use}}.

task.description: The specific subtask for {{specialist_name}}. Be concrete and self-contained — the specialist does not see prior conversation.

extension_rules: If specialist's input_contract has fields beyond `task` (e.g., findings, context), extend `input_schema.properties` accordingly and add to `required`.
If multiple specialists share overlapping use cases, the description must explicitly disambiguate — name the OTHER specialists and when to prefer each.
Per Pitfall #1 in make_orchestrator_agent.md, do NOT expose the specialist's underlying tools alongside delegate_to_*. The wrapper is the entire surface.

orchestrator_json_skeleton._description: The top-level structure the skill writes to <orchestrator_name>.json. Mirrors make_agent's emitted JSON shape plus the topology object.

agent_type.description: Orchestrator that delegates to {{specialist_count}} specialists via delegate_to_<name> tools. Specialists: {{specialist_names_csv}}.

known_failures.cause: spec_path resolved at generation time but specialist failed at runtime
known_failures.action: Halt and surface per P-003; do not fabricate specialist output.

cross_references.knowledge_files: {{knowledge_files_array_if_any — per-entry shape: { path: <.json path, NOT .md>, purpose: <one-line>, runtime_strategy: 'embed'|'read_at_runtime'|'platform_cached' }; see make_agent.json → cross_references._knowledge_files_format for the canonical doc-string}}

skeleton._dependencies_note: Orchestrators do NOT carry a top-level `dependencies` block (unlike make_agent's tier_1_core dependencies). The orchestrator's runtime dependencies are: (1) the specialist spec files listed in topology.specialists[].spec_path, and (2) any knowledge files in cross_references.knowledge_files[]. Both are already enumerated elsewhere — duplicating into a `dependencies` block would create drift. If an orchestrator has non-specialist non-knowledge dependencies (e.g., a python package the runner needs), add them inline in implementation.llm_agent or note them in operational_guidance instead.

pre_run_checklist: All specialist spec_path files exist and are readable
post_run_checklist: All delegate_to_* calls in trace resolved to tool_result
Termination criteria met or max_turns reached

changes: Initial orchestrator spec generated by make_orchestrator_agent v1.0

compact_boilerplate._description: Templates substituted into the generated orchestrator's system_prompt and MD sections. The most important is system_prompt_template — runtime LLMs read the system prompt, not the MD.
compact_boilerplate.topology_md_section_template:
## Topology

This orchestrator coordinates {{specialist_count}} specialists:

{{specialist_bullet_list}}

Each specialist is referenced by `spec_path` and invoked via its corresponding `delegate_to_<name>` tool. Specialists are stateless w.r.t. each other; cross-specialist state lives only in this orchestrator's context.

## Routing Decisions

{{routing_narrative_one_paragraph_per_specialist}}

When multiple specialists could plausibly handle a step, the orchestrator chooses based on each tool's `description`. Tool descriptions must therefore name the OTHER specialists they compete with and explain when to prefer each.

## Cross-Agent State

Orchestrator carries: user_request{{conditional_session_id}}{{conditional_knowledge_refs}}.
Each specialist sees: only the `task` argument passed in its `delegate_to_*` call.
If specialist B needs specialist A's output, the orchestrator MUST pass it explicitly in B's `task`.

## Termination

The orchestrator stops delegating when: **{{termination_criteria}}**

A backstop `max_turns: {{max_turns}}` catches the case where the criteria are never satisfied.
compact_boilerplate.platform_notes_template_xai:
## Platform Notes

**xAI exception**: this spec is portable across Anthropic, OpenAI, and Google ADK runners. xAI's multi-agent surface (Grok 4.20 Multi-Agent Beta) uses anonymous server-side workers chosen by `agent_count=4|16` and does not expose named specialists. To deploy on xAI, generate a single-agent spec via `make_agent` with model `grok-4.20-multi-agent` and the appropriate `agent_count` instead of using this orchestrator spec directly.

pre_run_checklist: Each specialist_paths[] entry exists and is readable
Each specialist .json parses as valid JSON and contains a behavioral_discipline.interaction_pattern field
orchestrator_name is unique within the project (no existing <name>.md or <name>.json at target path, or user has confirmed overwrite per P-002)
specialist_paths has length between 2 and 10 inclusive
termination_criteria is non-empty and at least 20 characters
post_run_checklist: <orchestrator_name>.md exists at target path with all 7 required sections in order
topology.specialists has one entry per specialist_path provided, in input order
implementation.llm_agent.tools has one delegate_to_<name> entry per specialist, no extras
Each delegate_to_* tool's _meta.specialist_spec_path resolves to a file that exists
topology.termination_criteria text appears verbatim in the system_prompt
max_turns is set as a backstop in implementation.llm_agent
behavioral_discipline block is embedded in both the MD (## Behavioral Discipline section) and the system_prompt
success_criteria: Generated orchestrator spec is portable across Anthropic / OpenAI / Google ADK runners (no platform-specific fields in the generic body)
All required sections present per orchestrator_md_required_sections
ORCH-QC-001 through ORCH-QC-005 pass (run via make_agent_qc)
BD-QC-001 through BD-QC-007 pass for the orchestrator agent itself

test_cases.name: Generate a 2-specialist research+writer orchestrator
test_cases.input: orchestrator_name=research_writer; specialist_paths=[specialists/researcher.json, specialists/writer.json]; interaction_pattern=multi_step_batch; io_mode=string; termination_criteria='Writer has returned a finished summary that addresses the user request.'
test_cases.expected_output: research_writer.md + research_writer.json created. topology.specialists has 2 entries. tools array has delegate_to_researcher + delegate_to_writer. system_prompt includes termination_criteria verbatim. ORCH-QC + BD-QC pass.

test_cases.name: Reject single-specialist orchestrator
test_cases.expected_output: ORCH-QC-002 fails before write. Skill explains: 'An orchestrator needs at least 2 specialists with a real routing decision. Call the specialist directly instead.' No files written.

test_cases.name: Reject silent overwrite of existing orchestrator file
test_cases.expected_output: Per P-002, skill proposes diff against existing file, waits for confirmation. Does NOT silently overwrite.

test_cases.name: Reject missing specialist spec_path
test_cases.expected_output: Pre-run checklist fails on the second path. P-001 (Read Before Claiming) halt — skill does not fabricate the missing specialist.

test_cases.name: xAI target deployment
test_cases.input: User requests an orchestrator spec for xAI deployment
test_cases.expected_output: Skill explains that xAI's multi-agent surface uses anonymous workers (no named specialists), points the user at make_agent with model=grok-4.20-multi-agent and agent_count, and does not generate an orchestrator spec.

qc_checks._description: Orchestrator-specific QC checks. These also live in make_agent_qc.json under the ORCH-QC family so they run as part of the unified QC pipeline. Listed here for skill-internal reference.

checks.name: All specialist spec_paths exist
checks.rule: Every topology.specialists[].spec_path resolves to a file that exists and is readable. P-001 (Read Before Claiming) — never reference a specialist that doesn't exist.

checks.name: Minimum 2 specialists
checks.rule: topology.specialists has at least 2 entries. A single-specialist orchestrator adds a layer of indirection with no routing decision — call the specialist directly instead.

checks.name: delegate_to_* tools match specialists
checks.rule: implementation.llm_agent.tools contains exactly one delegate_to_<specialist_name> entry per topology.specialists[]. No duplicates, no extras. Each tool's _meta.specialist_spec_path matches its corresponding specialist entry.

checks.name: Termination criteria present and restated
checks.rule: topology.termination_criteria is non-empty (≥ 20 chars) AND its text appears verbatim in implementation.llm_agent.system_prompt AND a max_turns backstop is set in implementation.llm_agent.parameters or runner config.

checks.name: No specialist underlying tools exposed
checks.rule: The orchestrator's implementation.llm_agent.tools array contains ONLY delegate_to_* wrappers (and any orchestrator-native tools like calculator that are NOT also in any specialist's tool list). Per P-007 and Pitfall #1 in make_orchestrator_agent.md, exposing specialist tools directly defeats the discipline boundary.

common_pitfalls_data._description: Structured data backing make_orchestrator_agent.md → Common Pitfalls section. Five pitfalls catalogued for traceability.

pitfalls.name: Toolifying Specialists Instead of Agentifying Them
Routing Logic In The System Prompt
Specialists Sharing An Implicit State Channel

changes: Initial make_orchestrator_agent skill — sibling to make_agent and make_AGENTS.
Generates orchestrator agent specs (.md + .json) with agent_type.type='multi_agent'.
Specialists declared by spec_path (file-paths-only design per scoping decision 2026-05-12).
Routing is LLM-routed via auto-generated delegate_to_<specialist> tools (per scoping decision 2026-05-12).
5 orchestrator-specific QC checks (ORCH-QC-001 through ORCH-QC-005). Also added to make_agent_qc.json as a unified pipeline.
Platform coverage: Anthropic, OpenAI, Google ADK as direct targets; xAI documented as exception (anonymous server-side workers; use make_agent with grok-4.20-multi-agent instead).
Grounded in source_docs/anthropic_subagents, openai_handoffs, openai_agents_class, openai_multi_agent, google_adk_multi_agents (refreshed 2026-05-12 / 2026-05-13).
